The '''Laos national football team''' ({{langx|lo|ທິມຊາດ ບານເຕະ ແຫ່ງຊາດ ລາວ}}) is the men's national [[association football|football]] team that represents the [[Laos]] in international football. It is governed by the [[Lao Football Federation]].

== History ==
Laos established their national football association in 1951. The Southeast Asian nation is still waiting to make its entrance into a major international competition. Laos have never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]], [[AFC Asian Cup]] or [[Asian Games]] and as an international side, their appearances have been restricted to regional tournaments such as the [[Southeast Asian Games]] and the [[AFF Championship]]. After years of internal strife, Laos focused on economic and political recovery. With the country achieving political stability, football has made an impact on Laotians.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.aseanfootball.org/v3/category/laos/|title=LFF hold knowledge exchange workshop with DFL|access-date=25 February 2020|archive-date=25 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225235733/https://www.aseanfootball.org/v3/category/laos/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.thailandoriginalmade.com/category/3301/2363/laos-national-team|title=Laos National Team - Thailandoriginalmade|access-date=25 February 2020|archive-date=25 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225235735/https://www.thailandoriginalmade.com/category/3301/2363/laos-national-team|url-status=live}}</ref>

Since making their appearance at the [[Football at the 1995 SEA Games|1995 Southeast Asian Games]], Laos has competed in the inaugural [[1996 AFF Championship]], drawing against [[Vietnam national football team|Vietnam]] 1–1 and winning against [[Cambodia national football team|Cambodia]] 1–0. Although they are new to the regional tournaments, Laos has displayed a fiery passion and talent. In 1995, they beat [[Brunei national football team|Brunei]] and [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]] and two years later in the [[Football at the 1997 SEA Games|1997 Southeast Asian Games]] held in Jakarta, they also beat [[Malaysia national football team|Malaysia]] 1–0 and [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]] 4–1. Domestic competitions are also active with over 60 clubs competing at various levels. Domestic football is amateur although most of the top teams are drawn from government ministries and public services. In the qualifying preliminary rounds for the [[2004 AFC Asian Cup|2004 Asian Cup]], Laos beat [[Bangladesh national football team|Bangladesh]] 2–1. In the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2006 FIFA World Cup qualification]], Laos qualified for the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round|second round]] as a lucky loser after [[Guam national football team|Guam]] and [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]] both withdrew from the competition, but proceeded to lose all its games (against [[Qatar national football team|Qatar]], [[Iran national football team|Iran]] and [[Jordan national football team|Jordan]]).<ref>{{Cite web | url=https://www.thai2english.com/dictionary/Laos%20national%20football%20team.html | title=Laos national football team | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200706135311/https://www.thai2english.com/dictionary/Laos%20national%20football%20team.html | archive-date=6 July 2020 }}</ref> They also advanced to the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round|second round]] of the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2014 FIFA World Cup qualification]] for the [[2014 FIFA World Cup]], after defeating [[Cambodia national football team|Cambodia]] 8–6 on aggregate. In the second round, they lost to [[China national football team|China]] 13–3 on aggregate. Laos has defeated their much more established counterparts such as Brunei, Cambodia, Philippines, [[Singapore national football team|Singapore]] and Malaysia. Laos' first appearance in a continental tournament was in 2014, when they played at the [[2014 AFC Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/laos/option/ranking/|title=Laos national football team: FIFA ranking|website=www.11v11.com|access-date=25 February 2020|archive-date=25 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200225235730/https://www.11v11.com/teams/laos/option/ranking/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.worldfootball.net/teams/te13987/laos/|title=Laos|date=3 September 2015 |access-date=25 February 2020|archive-date=13 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201013130040/https://www.worldfootball.net/teams/laos-team/|url-status=live}}</ref> In 2016, Laos were invited to the inaugural [[2016 AFC Solidarity Cup]] held in [[Kuching]] being grouped with [[Macau national football team|Macau]], [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]] and [[Mongolia national football team|Mongolia]]. In the first match, Laos won Sri Lanka 2–0 before losing to Macau 1–4 but bounced back with another win against Mongolia 3–0 sending the team to the knockout stage. They would go on to face Nepal in the semi-finals and were 2–2 at the end of extra time but lost in the penalty shootout, thus bowing out from the cup.

In 2023, Laos participated in the first round of the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC first round|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]] facing against [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]]. On 12 October 2023, Laos contested in a 1–1 draw at the [[Dasharath Rangasala]] in [[Kathmandu]] but lost 1–0 at home suffering a 2–1 aggregate. Laos would then go on to play in the [[2024 ASEAN Championship]] after playing just two friendlies match against Malaysia and Thailand in November 2024 right before the tournament began.

==Team images==
===Kits===
{{Commons|Laos national football team kits}}
Laos's current kit sponsor is [[Jogarbola]]. The contract started in June 2025. Laos was also previously sponsored by [[Adidas]], [[FBT (company)|FBT]], ''Deffo'', ''Evo'' and [[Grand Sport Group|Grand Sport]]. The traditional home colour for the team is all red with white trim and the away colour is all white with red trim. Occasionally, the team wore dark blue short as home jersey and all blue as away jerseys.

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following 30 players were called up to prepare for the [[2026 ASEAN Championship]].<ref>{{cite news |title=ລາຍຊື່ນັກກິລາຄັດເລືອກ ທີມຊາດລາວ ຊຸດໃຫຍ່ |access-date=4 July 2026|publisher=Lao Football Federation |date=5 June 2026 |language=Lao|url=https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1EBQT9X1Uv/|via=Facebook}}</ref>

''Caps and goals as of 31 March 2026, after the match against [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]].''

<sup>INJ</sup> Withdrew from the squad due to injury<br>
<sup>PRE</sup> Included in the preliminary squad or on standby<br>
<sup>RET</sup> Retired from the team<br>
<sup>SUS</sup> Serving suspension from the team<br>
<sup>WD</sup> Withdrew from the squad due to non-injury issue<br>
{{nat fs end}}
